records.ety: [F. main hand + prise a taking, fr. prendre, p. p. pris to take, fr. L. prehendere, prehensum.]

defs: A writ directed to the sheriff, commanding him to take sureties, called mainpernors, for the prisoner's appearance, and to let him go at large. This writ is now obsolete.
Deliverance of a prisoner on security for his appearance at a day.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Mainprised (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Mainprising.]

defs: To suffer to go at large, on his finding sureties, or mainpernors, for his appearance at a day; -- said of a prisoner.
